zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Clifford於1142年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾利哈達斯 卡素時，他的父親Walter De Clifford29歲，母親Margaret de Tosny28歲。他於大約1176年與Letitia Berkeley在英國賀福爾克利福德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1213年在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 奧 澤福享年71歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦藍白斯聖 瑪麗 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Clifford was born in 1142, in Richard's Castle, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Walter De Clifford, was 29 and his mother, Margaret de Tosny, was 28. He married Letitia Berkeley about 1176, in Clifford, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1213, in Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71, and was buried in Saint Mary Churchyard, Lambeth, London, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Clifford è nato il nel 1142 nel Richard's Castle, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Walter De Clifford, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Margaret de Tosny, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Letitia Berkeley circa 1176, nel Clifford, Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1213, nel Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolto a Saint Mary Churchyard, Lambeth, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Cliffordが1142年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアリチャード'ス カストルで生まれた時，父Walter De Cliffordは29歳，母Margaret de Tosnyは28歳であった。約1176年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクリフォードでLetitia Berkeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1213年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフランプトン オン セバーンで71歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンランベスサイント マリー チャーチヤルドにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Mary Churchyard, Frampton-On-Sever: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 램스 세인트 메리 처르c히아어드
ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Clifford(이)가 1142년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 리처드'스 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter De Clifford(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Tosny(은)는 28세였다. 그는 대략 1176년 영국 허퍼드실르 클리퍼드에서 Letitia Berkeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1213년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴  세버에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 램스 세인트 메리 처르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Clifford nasceu em 1142, em Richard's Castle, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Walter De Clifford, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Tosny, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Letitia Berkeley aproximadamente 1176, em Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1213, em Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em Saint Mary Churchyard, Lambeth, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Saint Mary Churchyard, Frampton-On-Sever: сеинт мэри чурчярд, лэмбет, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.Frampton-On-Severn, Wheatenhurst, Glouce: фрамптон он сивирн, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Clifford родился в 1142 г. в местности ричард'с кастл, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Walter De Clifford было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Tosny, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Letitia Berkeley около 1176 г. в местности клиффорд, хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1213 г. в местности фрамптон он сивирн, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности сеинт мэри чурчярд, лэмбет, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Clifford nació en 1142, en Richard's Castle, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Walter De Clifford, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Margaret de Tosny, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Letitia Berkeley aproximadamente 1176, en Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1213, en Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Saint Mary Churchyard, Lambeth, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
